title: Cron loop and job dispatcher
description:
Implement APScheduler-based cron loop that dispatches pipeline stages on configured intervals. Entry point for the long-running agent process.

## Design

- **BlockingScheduler** (not Background) — this IS the main process. Handles SIGINT/SIGTERM internally.
- **Thin wrappers** (`run_stage_3`, etc.) with lazy imports — fast startup, import errors in one stage don't kill others, easy to mock.
- **`_run_stage(name, func)`** — generic logging wrapper: logs start/finish/elapsed/result, catches unexpected exceptions.
- **`replace_existing=True`** on all jobs — safe restart with persistent SQLAlchemy job store.
- **`process_input_sheet()`** stays as-is, becomes a scheduled job.

## Files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `app/base/config.py` | Add `SchedulerSettings` with interval defaults |
| `app/base/scheduler.py` | Switch `BackgroundScheduler` → `BlockingScheduler` |
| Entrypoint module (TBD) | `_run_stage` wrapper, `run_stage_*`, `register_jobs`, `main()` |
| Entrypoint test (TBD) | Tests for `_run_stage`, `register_jobs`, and each wrapper |

## Open questions

1. **Entrypoint location** — `app/agent/main.py` is a stub. Options: keep it there, move to `app/main.py`, or a top-level `run_scheduler.py` alongside `run_pipeline.py`.
2. **Intervals** — what cadence for each stage?
close_reason: Implemented: two cron-aligned pipelines (sourcing */15, negotiation */30), _fan_out with ThreadPoolExecutor, _run_stage wrapper, BlockingScheduler, tests. Entry point at app/main.py.

title: Streamlit Observability Dashboard
description: Streamlit dashboard for real-time pipeline and agent observation. Separate lightweight Docker service reading from shared Postgres and log volume. Pages: Overview (pipeline health, thread funnel, error badge), Products (source products + supplier matches), Threads (lifecycle tracking, conversation history, quote history, links to Kogan/supplier URLs and Gmail), Logs (tail any log file, filter by stage/thread/source).

title: Create dashboard directory structure and DB module
description: Create dashboard/ with: db.py (standalone DB connection using same PG env vars, no app/ imports — avoids pulling Browserbase/Playwright/Gmail deps), queries.py (raw SQL via sqlalchemy.text() for all dashboard queries), app.py (Streamlit entry point with sidebar nav), pages/ directory. Also create Dockerfile (python:3.12-slim + streamlit/sqlalchemy/psycopg[binary]/plotly only, ~200MB) and requirements.txt.

title: Overview page
description: Page 1: Pipeline health at a glance. Thread state count cards. Thread state funnel visualization (NEW→OUTREACH_SENT→...→FINAL_PRICE_LOGGED with conversion rates). Recent activity feed (last 20 lines from app.log). Error count badge (last 24h from errors.log). Auto-refresh every 30s.

title: Products page
description: Page 2: All source products table (ID, title, Kogan URL as link, spec group count, supplier product count, thread count, created_at). Click-through shows supplier products sub-table (supplier name, platform, product URL as link, price, MOQ, match status).

title: Threads page
description: Page 3: Core operational view. Filterable by state (multiselect), source product, date range. Table: thread ID, source product title, supplier name, state (color badges), negotiation rounds, last_updated, respond_after, Gmail link, Kogan URL, supplier URL. Detail panel on select: full message history (direction-colored), quote history (price/MOQ/lead_time per round), all relevant links.

title: Logs page
description: Page 4: Real-time log viewer. Dropdown to select log file (app.log, errors.log, s1-s6). Tail last N lines (configurable, default 100). Text search filter. Context filter by stage/thread_id/source_url using structured log format. ERROR/EXCEPTION lines highlighted red. Auto-refresh toggle (5s interval).

title: Add dashboard service to docker-compose
description: Add dashboard service to docker-compose.yml: builds from dashboard/Dockerfile, depends on db (healthy), reads same .env, PG_HOST=db, mounts applogs volume as read-only at /logs, exposes port 8501, restart always. The logging epic's applogs volume is a prerequisite.

title: Platform messaging: read and reply to on-platform supplier messages
description:
Suppliers are replying via Alibaba's platform messaging instead of email. Need to:
1. Extract shared BrowseToolkit from inquiry_agent (browse CLI, screenshot, i18n stripping, finish tool, classify_from_history)
2. Refactor inquiry_agent to use BrowseToolkit
3. Build deterministic Playwright scripts for Alibaba message center (read inbox, send reply) in alibaba/messaging.py
4. Build fallback platform_message_agent using BrowseToolkit (same pattern as inquiry_agent)
5. Add messaging_agent_prompt to platform protocol and Alibaba Platform class
6. Integrate platform message polling into Stage 4 (deterministic + agent fallback)
7. Make Stage 5 channel-agnostic (reply_fn closure, never sees Gmail or platform directly)

DB changes already done: channel + platform_thread_url columns on supplier_threads and messages.

title: Extract BrowseToolkit from inquiry_agent into shared module
description:
Create app/pipeline/agents/browse_tools.py with BrowseToolkit class:
- run_browse(), screenshot(), browse() bound to session_id
- _strip_i18n() for snapshot cleanup
- classify_from_history() generic fallback
- Finish tool factory (parameterized by result type + holder list) — needed because structured output doesn't work with thinking mode on Bedrock
- .tools() returns base PydanticAI Tool list

title: Refactor inquiry_agent to use BrowseToolkit
description: Replace inline _make_tools/_strip_i18n/BROWSE_BIN with BrowseToolkit. inquiry_agent adds only submit_inquiry_iframe on top. No behavioral change.

title: Deterministic Alibaba messaging (alibaba/messaging.py)
description: Playwright automation for Alibaba message center. read_platform_messages(page) navigates to message.alibaba.com, finds unread conversations, reads messages. send_platform_reply(page, conversation_url, message) opens conversation, types reply, sends. Primary path — agent fallback handles failures.

title: Platform message fallback agent
description: Rewrite platform_message_agent.py as fallback agent (same role as inquiry_agent). Generic system prompt + per-platform messaging_agent_prompt injected. Entry points: read_inbox_via_agent(session_id, platform_prompt) and send_reply_via_agent(session_id, conversation_url, message, platform_prompt). Uses BrowseToolkit, adds report_message tool for inbox reading.

title: Wire messaging into platform protocol and Alibaba Platform class
description: Add messaging_agent_prompt to SupplierPlatform protocol. Add read_platform_messages() and send_platform_reply() to protocol. Implement in Alibaba Platform class, delegating to messaging.py.

title: Integrate platform message polling into Stage 4
description: After Gmail polling, authenticate on platforms with active OUTREACH_SENT threads. Try deterministic read_platform_messages(), agent fallback on failure. Match messages to supplier threads by supplier name. Record inbound messages with channel=platform, store conversation URL on thread.

title: Make Stage 5 channel-agnostic
description: Refactor _process_thread to accept reply_fn closure instead of GmailService. process_negotiations() builds the right closure per thread channel. Email uses Gmail reply (existing). Platform uses deterministic send_platform_reply + agent fallback, auth cached per platform. _process_thread never imports GmailService or knows about channels.

title: File-Based Logging System
description: Comprehensive file-based logging with per-stage log files, error-specific logs with stack traces, and context linking (thread_id, source_url, supplier_url) for easy diagnosis. Replaces current basicConfig console-only logging with RotatingFileHandlers and ContextVar-based context injection.

title: Create app/base/logging.py module
description: New module with: ContextVars (stage, source_url, thread_id), set_log_context()/clear_log_context() helpers, ContextFilter (injects ContextVar values into LogRecords), StageMatchFilter (routes records to per-stage files), LogSettings(BaseSettings) for LOG_DIR/LOG_MAX_BYTES/LOG_BACKUP_COUNT, configure_logging() that creates handlers: console, app.log (INFO+ rotated 10MB x5), errors.log (ERROR+ rotated 10MB x10), s1-s6 per-stage files (each with StageMatchFilter).

title: Remove configure_logging from config.py, update imports
description: Remove configure_logging() from app/base/config.py (lines 95-99). Update imports in app/main.py and scripts/run_pipeline.py to use from app.base.logging import configure_logging.

title: Inject stage context in _run_stage and _fan_out
description: In app/main.py: _run_stage() — call set_log_context(stage=stage_name) before func(), clear_log_context() in finally. _fan_out._run() — call set_log_context(stage=stage_name) at top of each worker thread (ContextVar doesn't propagate to ThreadPoolExecutor threads).

title: Propagate context in stage internal ThreadPoolExecutors
description: Stages s2 and s3 have their own internal ThreadPoolExecutors where ContextVar is lost. Add set_log_context() calls in: s2_supplier_search.py _fetch_and_save_offer() — re-set stage + supplier_url. s2_supplier_search.py _match_single_candidate() — re-set stage. s3_outreach.py _send_single_inquiry() — re-set stage + thread_id + supplier_url. s5_negotiation.py _process_thread() — set thread_id + source_url.

title: Docker and gitignore updates for logging
description: docker-compose.yml — add applogs named volume, mount to /app/logs on app service. Add logs/ to .gitignore.

title: Unit tests for logging module
description: Test configure_logging creates expected handlers/filters. Test ContextFilter reads from ContextVars and enriches records. Test StageMatchFilter accepts/rejects correctly. Test set_log_context/clear_log_context. Pure unit tests, no external services.

title: Pipeline recovery: scheduled job to detect and re-dispatch stuck threads
description: Scheduled job that scans for threads stuck at each pipeline stage (e.g. never reached stage 2, stage 3, etc). Should detect failures across the whole pipeline and re-dispatch to the correct stage. Makes the pipeline truly self-healing/recoverable without manual intervention.
